tags.name: arcgis-ckeditor5
tags.description:
### Events
- **arcgisCkeditor5Created**
- **arcgisCkeditor5DataUpdated**
- **arcgisCkeditor5SourceEditingChanged**
- **arcgisCreated** - Emitted when the editor has been created.
- **arcgisDataUpdated** - Emitted when the editor's data has been updated.
- **arcgisSourceEditingChanged** - Emitted when the editor's source editing mode has changed.

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.
- `getData(): Promise<any>` - Get the current data in the editor.
- `setFocus(): Promise<void>` - Set focus on the component.

attributes.name: can-resize-vertically
attributes.description: The editor's text area will have a vertical resize handle if set to true

attributes.name: current-language
attributes.description: language for ckeditor

attributes.name: custom-config
attributes.description:
Config to customize editor (will be merged with default config)
Default config will match html or markdown editor here:https://devtopia.esri.com/WebGIS/ckeditor5-build-classic.
Also includes default language and field expression mentions settings

attributes.name: editor-type
attributes.description: Base configuration for editor

attributes.name: group-fields-and-expressions
attributes.description: The fields / expressions in the default mention configuration will be grouped if set to true

attributes.name: intl-expressions-group-heading
attributes.description: Header text for expressions mention dropdown (assuming default mention configuration)

attributes.name: intl-fields-group-heading
attributes.description: Header text for fields in mention dropdown (assuming default mention configuration)

attributes.name: intl-text-place-holder
attributes.description: Placeholder text for editor

attributes.name: read-only
attributes.description: The toolbar will be hidden and the data will only be changeable through the `textData` prop if true

attributes.name: text-data
attributes.description: Input data for editor in html / markdown text format

tags.name: arcgis-ckeditor5-popover
tags.description:
### Events
- **arcgisCkeditor5PopoverClosed**
- **arcgisPopoverClosed** - Emitted when the popover has been closed.

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.
- `reposition(): Promise<void>` - Reposition the component.

attributes.name: guid
attributes.description: Guid for ckeditor popover instance.

attributes.name: intl-cancel
attributes.description: Cancel button text.

attributes.name: intl-label
attributes.description: Popover label text.

attributes.name: intl-ok
attributes.description: Ok button text.

tags.name: arcgis-color-input
tags.description:
### Events
- **arcgisChange** - Emitted when the color has changed.
- **arcgisColorInputChange**
- **arcgisColorInputPopoverClose**
- **arcgisColorInputPopoverOpen**
- **arcgisColorInputSwatchUpdate**
- **arcgisPickerClose**
- **arcgisPickerOpen**
- **arcgisSwatchUpdate** - Emitted when a color swatch is updated (emits selected color hex).

### Methods
- `close(): Promise<void>` - Close the component.
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.
- `setFocus(): Promise<void>` - Set focus on the component.

attributes.name: clearable
attributes.description: Display option to set no color.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: The selected color as a HEX value. If not set it will display a 'transparent' color swatch.

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description: Control whether the component should be displayed in a compact style (i.e., fewer recent color options).

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Control whether the component is disabled.

attributes.name: hide-recent-colors
attributes.description: If true, do not display recent colors.

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description: Determines where the popup color picker will be positioned relative to the color input.

attributes.name: show-transparency
attributes.description: If true, allow transparency to be modified within the color picker.

attributes.name: storage-id
attributes.description: The id of the local storage element that will store recent colors.

tags.name: arcgis-field-info
tags.description:
### Events
- **arcgisComplete** - Emitted once all information is displayed.
- **arcgisFieldInfoComplete**

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.
- `refresh(): Promise<void>` - Refresh the field info display after external changes are made.

attributes.name: field-name
attributes.description: Required field name.

attributes.name: hide-alias
attributes.description: If true, hide the field alias section.

attributes.name: hide-numeric-format
attributes.description: If true, hide the decimal places section.

attributes.name: hide-statistics
attributes.description: If true, hide statistics (i.e., no top 10 values for string and no stats for number and date fields).

tags.name: arcgis-field-pick-list
tags.description:
A component that displays a list of fields for selection, inside a popover (default) or just as a list.
--

### Events
- **arcgisChange** - Emitted when the selected fields have changed. selectedFields contains fields in order of the user's selection.
- **arcgisClose** - Emitted when the pick list has been closed.
- **arcgisDeleteField** - Emitted when the delete icon of a field has been clicked. The field must have IPickListFieldInfo.showDeleteAction set to true.
- **arcgisEditField** - Emitted when the edit icon of a field has been clicked. The field must have IPickListFieldInfo.showEditAction set to true.
- **arcgisFieldPickListChange**
- **arcgisFieldPickListDismissed**
- **arcgisFieldPickListSortByChange**
- **arcgisSortByChange** - Emitted when the field pick list sort order has changed.

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.
- `reposition(): Promise<void>` - Reposition the component.
- `setDisabled(disable: boolean): Promise<void>` - Control whether the component is disabled.
- `setFocus(): Promise<void>` - Set focus on the component.

### Slots
- **content-top** - A slot for content on top of the search filter and list, non-scrollable. Valid only if listOnly is not set to true.
- **content-center** - A slot for content below the search filter and above the list items, will scroll with the list.
- **content-bottom** - A slot for content below the list, non-scrollable. Valid only if listOnly is not set to true.
- **content-footer** - A slot for content below the list inside the panel's footer, non-scrollable. Valid only if listOnly is not set to true.
- **no-fields** - A slot for content, e.g. a message, that displays if the list of fields is empty.

attributes.name: expressions-group-heading
attributes.description: Header text for group containing expressions, if 'groupFieldsAndExpressions' is set to true.

attributes.name: fields-group-heading
attributes.description: Header text for group containing fields, if 'groupFieldsAndExpressions' is set to true.

attributes.name: filter-no-results-text
attributes.description: Filter no results text. If empty string ("") no text will be shown.

attributes.name: filter-placeholder-text
attributes.description: Filter placeholder text.

attributes.name: group-fields-and-expressions
attributes.description:
**Deprecated**: Pass in grouped lists of fields instead.
If true, fields and expressions are placed in separate groups..

attributes.name: heading
attributes.description: Heading text. Valid only if listOnly is not set to true.

attributes.name: hide-cancel
attributes.description: If true, hides the cancel button. Valid only if listOnly is not set to true.

attributes.name: hide-sort
attributes.description: If true, hides the sort menu button.

attributes.name: list-only
attributes.description: If true, renders without a popover.

attributes.name: multiple
attributes.description:
If true, allows for multiple selection. If false, the
popover will close once an item has been selected.

attributes.name: ok-btn-text
attributes.description: Ok button text. Ok button only shows when multiple set to true. Valid only if listOnly is not set to true.

attributes.name: show-cancel
attributes.description: **Deprecated**: use hideCancel instead.

attributes.name: show-description
attributes.description: If true, displays description. By default only displays field alias.

attributes.name: show-field-info
attributes.description: If true, displays the field info button. Valid only if listOnly is not set to true.

attributes.name: show-field-name
attributes.description: If true, displays field name. By default only displays field alias.

attributes.name: show-field-type-icon
attributes.description: If true, displays an icon corresponding to the field's type.

attributes.name: show-filter-length
attributes.description: If true, displays the filter option based on the number of fields.

attributes.name: show-selection-all
attributes.description: If true, displays a 'Select All' button on top of the list. Valid only if multiple is set to true.

attributes.name: show-sort
attributes.description: **Deprecated**: use hideSort instead.

attributes.name: sort-by
attributes.description: The initial sort order.

tags.name: arcgis-histogram
tags.description:
Renders a histogram to visualize the spread of a dataset based on [bins](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#bins) representing buckets, or sub-ranges, of
data. Each [bin](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/smartMapping/statistics/types/#HistogramBin) is defined by a minimum and maximum value and a total count.

You can generate the underlying histogram's bins with the [histogram](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/smartMapping/statistics/histogram/)
module, then apply them to the component, as shown in the example below.

```js
const params = {
  layer: povLayer,
  field: "POP_POVERTY",
  normalizationField: "TOTPOP_CY",
  numBins: 30
};

const results = await histogram(params);
const histogramElement = document.querySelector("arcgis-histogram");

histogramElement.bins = results.bins;
histogramElement.min = results.minValue;
histogramElement.max = results.maxValue;
```

Other properties of this component allow you to display meaningful values on the histogram, such as the [average](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#average),
[standardDeviation](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#standardDeviation), and the [dataLines](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#dataLines) properties.

The [layout](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#layout) property allows you to set the orientation of the histogram, and the [colorStops](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#colorStops)
property allows you to set a gradient or color scheme for the histogram bars.

See the image below for a summary of the configurable options available on the histogram.

![Histogram with annotations](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/assets/references/core/widgets/histogram-basic-labels.png "Histogram with annotations")

The [colorStops](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#colorStops) property may be used to define a color scheme for the histogram bars.

![Histogram with color](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/assets/references/core/widgets/histogram-colors.png "Histogram with color")
--

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.

### CSS Properties
- **--arcgis-histogram-bar-color** - Specifies the color of the histogram bars when the bin doesn't have a corresponding color stop. 
- **--arcgis-histogram-stop-color** - Specifies the default color for color stops.

attributes.name: average
attributes.description:
The statistical average of the data in the histogram. You would typically
get this value from the `avg` property of
[SummaryStatisticsResult](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/smartMapping/statistics/summaryStatistics/#SummaryStatisticsResult),
which is the result of the
[summaryStatistics](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/smartMapping/statistics/summaryStatistics/#summaryStatistics) function.

When set, this value will render on the histogram with a symbol indicating it is the average.

attributes.name: color-blending-enabled
attributes.description:
When set to `true`, overlapping classed color stops will be blended together to create the color of the bar.
This allows to show a if a bar representing a bin overlaps with multiple classed color stops.

This property is only applicable when the [colorStops](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#colorStops) property is set to classed color stops.

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description:
Icon which represents the component.
Typically used when the component is controlled by another component (e.g. by the Expand component).
Search [Calcite Icons](https://developers.arcgis.com/calcite-design-system/icons/) for possible values.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The component's default label.

attributes.name: layout
attributes.description: Determines the orientation of the histogram.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description:
The maximum value or bound of the entire histogram. This
should match the maximum bound of the last [bins](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#bins).

attributes.name: min
attributes.description:
The minimum value or bound of the entire histogram. This
should match the minimum bound of the first [bins](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/#bins).

attributes.name: standard-deviation
attributes.description:
The statistical standard deviation of the data in the histogram. You would typically
get this value from the `stddev` property of
[SummaryStatisticsResult](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/smartMapping/statistics/summaryStatistics/#SummaryStatisticsResult),
which is the result of the
[summaryStatistics](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/smartMapping/statistics/summaryStatistics/#summaryStatistics) function.

When set, this value will render on the histogram with symbols indicating standard deviation intervals from the mean.

tags.name: arcgis-label-input
tags.description:
### Events
- **arcgisChange** - Fires each time a new value is typed and committed.

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.
- `edit(): Promise<void>` - Initiates the edit mode for the component.

attributes.name: alignment
attributes.description: Specifies the alignment of the component's text elements.

attributes.name: editing
attributes.description:
Indicates whether the component is in edit mode.
This value changes when user interacts with the component or when [edit()](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-label-input/#edit) is called.
The value is always `false` when the component is in [readOnly](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-label-input/#readOnly) mode.

attributes.name: read-only
attributes.description: Indicates whether the component is in read-only mode.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The value the component displays.

attributes.name: heading

attributes.name: icon-end
attributes.description: Icon to display at the end of the component.

attributes.name: intl-done

attributes.name: label

tags.name: arcgis-slider
tags.description:
The Slider component is used to filter data or collect numeric input from users. It supports single or multiple thumbs, configured via the
[values](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#values) property. The Slider can be displayed in horizontal or vertical [layout](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#layout), supports snapping to defined [steps](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#steps),
configurable value [precision](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#precision), and custom [labelFormatter](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#labelFormatter). It also includes keyboard interaction,
optional editable labels, and customizable popover content.

The slider component can be set up with the [arcgis-ticks](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/) component. See the example below:
```html
<arcgis-slider min="0" max="100" show-range-labels value-labels-display="always" steps="1" value-labels-placement="start">
  <arcgis-ticks interactive slot="content-end" style="flex:1;" min="0" max="100" mode="count" values="11" show-labels>
  </arcgis-ticks>
</arcgis-slider>
```

### Which slider should you use: arcgis-slider or calcite-slider?
>
> In general, use [calcite-slider](https://developers.arcgis.com/calcite-design-system/components/slider/) over `arcgis-slider`.
> It covers common slider use cases and works well in most scenarios.
>
> Use `arcgis-slider` only when you need functionality that `calcite-slider` does not provide, such as:
> * [layout](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#layout): `arcgis-slider` supports `layout="vertical"`.
> * Custom range display: `arcgis-slider` supports [fullRangeMin](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#fullRangeMin) and [fullRangeMax](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#fullRangeMax) for displaying a wider
> range than the interactive range.
> * More than two values: `arcgis-slider` supports an arbitrary number of slider values, beyond single-value or range (two-value) sliders.
> * Slot support: Use the `content-start`, `content-end`, and `popover` slots to render custom content before the track, after the track, or inside the popover.
--

### Events
- **arcgisActiveValueChange** - Fires when the [activeValue](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#activeValue) changes as a thumb or the range gains or loses focus.
This event does not fire when [values](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#values) change. Use [@arcgisInput](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#event-arcgisInput) for continuous updates or
[@arcgisChange](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#event-arcgisChange) when the interaction is committed.
- **arcgisChange** - Fires when the thumb or range is released on the component.

Use [@arcgisInput](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#event-arcgisInput) for continuous updates during a drag.
- **arcgisInput** - Fires continuously while the thumb or range is being dragged.
This event can fire frequently; consider debouncing or throttling expensive work.
- **arcgisRangeChange** - Fires when the slider's [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) and [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max) range changes.
This occurs when the user edits the [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) or [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max) range labels (when enabled).

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.
- `destroy(): Promise<void>` - Permanently destroys the component.

### Slots
- **content-start** - A slot for elements before the track.
- **content-end** - A slot for elements after the track.
- **popover** - A slot for custom content to be rendered in the popover.

attributes.name: allow-values-overlap
attributes.description:
When `true`, allows multiple thumbs to overlap by sharing the same value.
When `false`, thumbs are prevented from overlapping.

attributes.name: auto-destroy-disabled
attributes.description:
If true, the component will not be destroyed automatically when it is
disconnected from the document. This is useful when you want to move the
component to a different place on the page, or temporarily hide it. If this
is set, make sure to call the [destroy()](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#destroy) method when you are done to
prevent memory leaks.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the slider is disabled.

attributes.name: fill-placement
attributes.description:
Controls where the filled segment is displayed on the track for single-thumb sliders.
Range sliders always display the fill between the min and max thumbs.

attributes.name: full-range-max
attributes.description:
Sets the maximum value of the slider display full range.
When specified, the slider track is scaled from [fullRangeMin](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#fullRangeMin) to `fullRangeMax`, while
the thumbs remain constrained to the interactive range defined by [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) and [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max).

The `fullRangeMax` must be greater than or equal to [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max) property of the slider.

attributes.name: full-range-min
attributes.description:
Sets the minimum value of the slider display full range.
When specified, the slider track is scaled from `fullRangeMin` to [fullRangeMax](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#fullRangeMax), while
the thumbs remain constrained to the range defined by [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) and [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max).

The `fullRangeMin` must be less than or equal to [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) property of the slider.

attributes.name: keyboard-large-step
attributes.description:
Controls how many "small" keyboard increments are applied in order to move a thumb or range faster using the
keyboard. The following keys are considered "large" increment keys:

- PageUp/PageDown
- Shift + Arrow keys

attributes.name: layout
attributes.description:
Determines the layout/orientation of the Slider component. By default, the slider will render horizontally with the min value on the left side
of the track.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description:
The maximum value for the slider thumbs. Thumbs will not move past this value.

To display the `max` label on the slider, set [showRangeLabels](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#showRangeLabels) to `true`.
To allow the end user to modify the `max` value, set [rangeLabelsEditingEnabled](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#rangeLabelsEditingEnabled) to `true`.

attributes.name: min
attributes.description:
The minimum value for the slider thumbs. Thumbs will not move past this value.
To display the `min` label on the slider, set [showRangeLabels](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#showRangeLabels) to `true`.
To allow the end user to modify the `min` value, set [rangeLabelsEditingEnabled](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#rangeLabelsEditingEnabled) to `true`.

attributes.name: mirrored
attributes.description: When `true`, the slider will display values from high to low. This inverts the direction of the slider.

attributes.name: popover-label
attributes.description: The accessible label for the popover. This label is used by assistive technologies and does not affect the visible UI.

attributes.name: popover-placement
attributes.description:
Determines which side of the track the popover is placed on.
The popover is the floating container that displays the content from the `popover` slot.
* `"start"`: Positions the popover above a horizontal slider or to the left of a vertical slider.
* `"end"`: Positions the popover below a horizontal slider or to the right of a vertical slider.

attributes.name: precision
attributes.description:
Defines how slider thumb values should be rounded. This number indicates the number
of decimal places slider thumb _values_ should round to when they have been moved.

This value also indicates the precision of thumb labels when the data range
is less than `10` (i.e. `(max - min) < 10`).

When the data range is larger than `10`, labels display with a precision of
no more than two decimal places, though actual slider thumb values will maintain the
precision specified in this property.

For example, given the default precision of `4`, and the following slider configuration,
The labels of the thumbs will display two decimal places, but the precision of the actual
thumb values will not be lost even when the user slides or moves the thumb.

Keep in mind this property rounds thumb [values](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#values) and shouldn't be used exclusively
for formatting purposes. To format thumb `labels`, use the [labelFormatter](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#labelFormatter)
property.

attributes.name: range-labels-editing-enabled
attributes.description:
When `true`, users can edit the [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) and [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max) range labels via keyboard input.
Editing these labels updates the slider’s interactive range.

attributes.name: range-labels-placement
attributes.description:
Controls where the [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) and [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max) range labels are positioned relative to the track.
This property only affects the slider when [layout](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#layout) is `"horizontal"`.

- `"center"` (default): Places the [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) and [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max) labels aligned with the track.
- `"end"`: Places the range labels below the the track.

attributes.name: segments-dragging-disabled
attributes.description: Indicates if the user can drag the segment between thumbs to update thumb positions.

attributes.name: show-range-labels
attributes.description: Indicates whether to [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) and [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max) range labels are visible on the slider.

attributes.name: steps
attributes.description:
Sets steps, or intervals, on the slider that restrict user
input to specific values. If an array of numbers is passed to this
property, the slider thumbs may only be moved to the positions
specified in the array.

If a single number is set, then steps are set along the entire
slider range at an interval of the provided value. In this scenario,
the user may only slide the thumbs to values at the provided interval.
For example, if a value of `0.5` is set here, and the slider
[min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#min) is `0` and the slider [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/#max) is `10`, then the user will
only be able to update the thumbs to values of 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, etc.

attributes.name: value-labels-display
attributes.description:
Controls when thumb value labels are displayed.

This property can be set to one of the following values:
- `"always"`: Always show the labels.
- `"auto"`: Show the labels only when the thumb representing a value is dragged.
- `"hidden"`: Never show the labels.

attributes.name: value-labels-editing-enabled
attributes.description:
Indicates whether to enable editing input values via keyboard input
when the user clicks a thumb value label. This allows the user to move the slider
thumb to precise values without sliding the thumbs.

![Slider with editable labels](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/assets/references/core/widgets/sliders/slider-edit-label.png)

attributes.name: value-labels-placement
attributes.description: Determines whether the thumb value labels are placed before or after the track.

attributes.name: values
attributes.description: An array of numbers representing absolute thumb positions on the slider.

tags.name: arcgis-slider-input
tags.description:
### Events
- **arcgisSliderInputValueChange**
- **arcgisValueChange** - Emitted when the value has changed.

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: If true, prevents interaction with the component.

attributes.name: flip-labels
attributes.description: Handle LTR and RTL label placement.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Label for the component.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description: Max value for the component.

attributes.name: min
attributes.description: Min value for the component.

attributes.name: step
attributes.description: Increment interval for the component.

attributes.name: units-label
attributes.description: If provided, specifies units for the input.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Initial value for the component.

attributes.name: with-steppers
attributes.description: If true, adds up/down arrows to the input.

tags.name: arcgis-ticks
tags.description:
The ticks component displays visual markers along a line to indicate scale, intervals, or specific values. It is often used in conjunction with the
[Slider component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/). Ticks help users understand the
slider’s range and make more precise selections.

Tick placement is controlled by the [mode](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#mode), which defines how the [values](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#values) are parsed and translated into positions along the
line. Tick positions are derived from the component’s [min](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#min) and [max](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#max) range, with optional none-linear scaling via
[interpolationExponent](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#interpolationExponent). Ticks can be displayed in vertical or horizontal [layout](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#layout), with optional [showLabels](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#showLabels) and a [showBaseline](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#showBaseline).
The component can also be made [interactive](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#interactive) to respond to user clicks on individual ticks. The [labelFormatter](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#labelFormatter) property allows customization of tick label formatting.
--

### Events
- **arcgisTickClick** - Fires when the user clicks on one of the ticks. This event is only emitted when the [interactive](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#interactive) property is set to `true`.

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.

### CSS Properties
- **--arcgis-ticks-tick-length** - Specifies the length of the ticks. 
- **--arcgis-ticks-tick-color** - Specifies the color of the ticks. 
- **--arcgis-ticks-gap** - Specifies the gap between the ticks and labels.

attributes.name: interactive
attributes.description: When `true`, ticks are clickable and the component emits  [@arcgisTickClick](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#event-arcgisTickClick) when the user selects a tick.

attributes.name: interpolation-exponent
attributes.description:
The exponent used to interpolate the position of ticks along the component.
A value of `0` indicates linear interpolation. Positive values indicate exponential interpolation, and negative values indicate logarithmic interpolation.

Set this property when the range of values is large and users need precision in the lower range of values (for an exponential interpolation), or in the higher range (for a logarithmic interpolation).

```tsx
<arcgis-ticks interpolation-exponent={14} min={1} max={5518000} mode="value" values={[1, 11500, 5518000]} />
```

attributes.name: label-placement
attributes.description: Determines whether the labels are placed before or after the ticks.

attributes.name: layout
attributes.description:
Determines the layout/orientation of the ticks component. By default, the ticks will render horizontally.
When set to `vertical`, the ticks will render vertically.

attributes.name: max
attributes.description: The component's maximum rendered value.

attributes.name: min
attributes.description: The component's minimum rendered value.

attributes.name: mirrored
attributes.description: When `true`, the values are displayed from high to low.

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description:
The mode or method of positioning ticks along the component. It drives how [values](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#values) is interpreted.
- `count`: Places a fixed number of ticks (provided in the [values](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#values) property) at equal distances from each other.
- `percent`: [values](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#values) is interpreted as percentage. If [values](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#values) is a number, it indicates the interval between ticks. If it is an array, it indicates the percentage values at which to place the ticks.
- `value`: Indicates that ticks will only be placed at the values specified in the property.

attributes.name: show-baseline
attributes.description: Indicates whether to render a line connecting the ticks.

attributes.name: show-labels
attributes.description: Indicates whether to render labels for the ticks.

attributes.name: values
attributes.description:
Indicates where ticks will be rendered along the component.
See the description for [mode](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/#mode) for more information about how this property is interpreted by each mode.

tags.name: arcgis-unique-values-list
tags.description:
Displays a list of unique values taking into account field type formatting and field domains.
--

### Events
- **arcgisChange** - Emitted if selection has changed
- **arcgisLoad** - Emitted once the component is loaded. Useful when no uniqueValues were passed in.
- **arcgisSortByChange** - Emitted if the sort order has changed

### Methods
- `componentOnReady(): Promise<this>` - Creates a promise that resolves once the component is fully loaded.
- `getUniqueValues(): Promise<{
    uniqueValues: __esri.UniqueValuesResultUniqueValueInfos[];
    partialUniqueValues: boolean;
} | undefined>` - Get all unique values.
E.g. to be used to limit how often these unique values need to be requested from the layer.
- `setFocus(): Promise<void>` - Sets focus on component

attributes.name: field-name
attributes.description: Field name, required if uniqueValues is not provided, if component needs to format values, or needs to check for field domains.

attributes.name: field-source
attributes.description: Optional, specify field source other than the layer's fields.

attributes.name: max-height
attributes.description: Optional, max height of list in pixel.

attributes.name: multiple
attributes.description: Multiple selections allowed?

attributes.name: partial-unique-values
attributes.description: Too many values? If true then it displays a message below the list. Only required if uniqueValues is provided.

attributes.name: show-filter-length
attributes.description: If true, displays the filter option based on the number of fields.

attributes.name: sort-by
attributes.description: Sort by default setting
